name: Sirloin Stuffed Spaghetti Squash
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Top sirloin steak, cut to 1/2 inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Halve the spaghetti squash lengthwise and scoop out the seeds. Place cut side down in a shallow dish with 1/2 inch of water. Microwave until fork-tender, about 15 minutes.
2. While the squash cooks, chop onion, green bell pepper, and tomatoes.
3. In a small bowl, mix oregano, thyme, red pepper flakes, and garlic salt.
4. Press the seasoning mixture onto both sides of the steak.
5. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Cook the steak for 3-5 minutes per side until browned.
6. Add the chopped vegetables to the skillet during the last 5 minutes of cooking.
7. Remove the steak and vegetables from the skillet, then cut the steak into 1/2-inch cubes.
8. Once the squash is tender, use a fork to pull it apart into spaghetti-like strands. Season with additional garlic salt and pepper if desired.
9. Divide the cooked squash among serving plates. Top each half with an equal portion of the seasoned steak and vegetable mixture. Spoon any pan juices over the squash halves. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
10. Bake in a preheated 350-degree oven for 8 to 10 minutes, or until heated through.
Serving option: Toss the cooked squash with prepared spaghetti or marinara sauce before baking, or serve with heated sauce after baking.
